prompt:
<|system|>You are an agent that interprets natural-language text and converts its meaning into a constrained OWL 2 output. The input text may contain Markdown or HTML formatting, including headers, emphasis, lists, and image tags. Treat Markdown and HTML purely as structural markup unless they contain meaningful content. Guidelines: - Use section headers (e.g., "#", "##") as meaningful indicators of topics or concepts. - Ignore formatting-only tags such as

, , , or layout-related HTML. - Ignore image tags except for their alt text, which may contain useful descriptive information. - Some HTML attributes or tag contents may encode semantic information (e.g., labels, identifiers, or descriptive values). When such information contributes meaning relevant to the ontology, extract it. - Do not treat markup syntax itself as ontology content. You must return exactly one JSON object that conforms to the following output schema. Do not return any text before or after the JSON object. The JSON object must contain exactly these fields: - "ontology" - "instances" Field requirements: - "ontology" must contain only TBox axiom objects. - "instances" must contain only ABox axiom objects. Allowed ontology object types: - {"type": "DeclareClass", "id": "LocalName"} - {"type": "DeclareObjectProperty", "id": "LocalName"} - {"type": "DeclareDataProperty", "id": "LocalName"} - {"type": "SubClassOf", "subclass": "LocalName", "superclass": "LocalName"} - {"type": "ObjectPropertyDomain", "property": "LocalName", "class": "LocalName"} - {"type": "ObjectPropertyRange", "property": "LocalName", "class": "LocalName"} Allowed instance object types: - {"type": "DeclareIndividual", "id": "LocalName"} - {"type": "ClassAssertion", "individual": "LocalName", "class": "LocalName"} - {"type": "ObjectPropertyAssertion", "subject": "LocalName", "property": "LocalName", "object": "LocalName"} - {"type": "DataPropertyAssertion", "subject": "LocalName", "property": "LocalName", "value": "text", "datatype": "xsd:string|xsd:integer|xsd:boolean"} Identifier rule: - Use simple local names only. Ontology construction rules: - Identify the key concepts and represent them as classes only when supported by the text. - Identify relationships and represent them as object properties only when supported by the text. - Identify literal-valued attributes and represent them as data properties only when supported by the text. - Identify concrete entities and represent them as individuals only when the text refers to specific instances rather than only general concepts. Grounding rules: - Keep the ontology grounded strictly in the provided text. - Avoid inventing domain knowledge, background facts, or vocabulary not supported by the text. - Do not add boilerplate prefixes, metadata, or standard vocabularies. Output rules: - Use only the allowed JSON object shapes inside the "ontology" and "instances" arrays. - Put schema-level knowledge in "ontology" and instance-level facts in "instances". - Ensure the final output is valid JSON and contains no commentary. <|end|><|user|>

Lykkegaard and Morten KjÃ¦rgaard. The company develops medical sensor products, based on advanced sensor technology, for monitoring of physical activity with patients. The SENS products and platforms are easy to use, flexible, scalable and present accurate monitoring of the body's position and the nature of the activity, for both the individual or even larger groups. Therefore, SENS's products and technology are a valuable tool in research trials and studies in correlation to physical activity and diseases. {{< /about-background >}}<|end|><|assistant|>
